seo.title: LangChain Guardrails for Healthcare
seo.description: Compliance guide for LangChain agents in healthcare. Meet HIPAA, FDA 21 CFR Part 11 requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Healthcare teams building with LangChain face immense risks when chaining LLMs for patient triage, drug interaction checks, or RAG-based medical record retrieval, as hallucinations, tool loops, and context overflows can lead to misdiagnoses or PHI breaches. HIPAA, HITECH, and FDA regulations demand auditable AI decisions and strict data containment, which LangChain's dynamic nature often undermines without guardrails. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure compliant, reliable LLM chains in high-stakes healthcare deployments.

complianceRequirements.description: Protects PHI from unauthorized disclosure; patient data cannot leave secure environments or be processed by unverified LLMs.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex verifies no PHI leakage in chain outputs and enforces de-identification before LLM processing with auditable traces.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires electronic records and signatures to be trustworthy, reliable, and auditable for AI-assisted medical decisions.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex captures immutable audit logs of every chain invocation, input/output validation, and decision provenance.

complianceRequirements.description: Strengthens HIPAA with breach notifications and higher fines; mandates risk assessments for AI handling PHI.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex runtime monitors detect hallucination or formatting failures, preventing breaches with automatic alerting.

complianceRequirements.description: Ensures security, availability, and privacy controls for healthcare SaaS handling patient data.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex provides continuous compliance monitoring and evidence collection for SOC 2 audits across LangChain deployments.

industryRisks.name: Hallucinated Drug Interactions
industryRisks.description: RAG chains retrieving from medical databases invent dangerous interactions due to poor retrieval grounding.
industryRisks.example: LangChain recommends metformin with acute kidney injury, causing patient harm and $1.5M HIPAA fine.

industryRisks.name: PHI Leakage in Agent Tools
industryRisks.description: Agent executors call external APIs with unredacted patient names/SSNs during diagnosis workflows.
industryRisks.example: Tool call exposes John Doe's full medical history to third-party lab API, triggering HITECH breach notification.

industryRisks.name: Context Overflow in Consult Chains
industryRisks.description: Long patient histories exceed token limits, truncating critical allergy data mid-conversation.
industryRisks.example: Penicillin allergy missed in ER triage chain, leading to anaphylaxis and malpractice liability.

industryRisks.name: Inconsistent Triage Formatting
industryRisks.description: Chain steps output varying JSON structures, breaking downstream EHR integration.
industryRisks.example: Urgent case scored as 'low' due to malformed output, delaying stroke treatment.

implementation.title: Wrap RAG Chain for Hallucination + PHI Guardrails
implementation.description: Verify medical record retrieval chains prevent hallucination and PHI leakage using Vex policies.
implementation.code:
from langchain.chains import RetrievalQA
from vex import verify

rag_chain = RetrievalQA.from_chain_type(llm=llm, retriever=vectorstore.as_retriever())

@verify(policies=["no-phi", "no-hallucination", "medical-accuracy"])
def compliant_rag(query: str, patient_id: str) -> str:
    # De-identify before LLM
    redacted_query = deidentify_phi(query, patient_id)
    result = rag_chain.invoke({"query": redacted_query})
    return result["result"]

response = compliant_rag("Patient allergies?", "12345")

caseStudyScenario.heading: LangChain in Healthcare: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
City General Hospital deployed a LangChain RAG system for ER triage, pulling from 10M+ patient records to prioritize cases. Agents called pharmacy APIs for drug checks while conversation chains maintained consult history across shifts. Without guardrails, the system hallucinated critical allergies (2% error rate), leaked PHI to external tools during loops, overflowed context on complex cases, and output inconsistent JSON that broke EHR integration.

During a flu surge, the system misclassified a septic patient as 'low risk' due to truncated history, delayed treatment by 4 hours, and exposed 500+ patient SSNs via faulty tool calls. Regulators issued a $1.2M HIPAA fine and mandated system shutdown.
caseStudyScenario.outcome:
Vex guardrails wrapped all chain.invoke() calls with PHI de-identification, hallucination detection, loop limits, and JSON schema enforcement. Hallucination incidents dropped to 0.1%, PHI breaches eliminated, and 100% of decisions became FDA Part 11 audit-ready.

The hospital processed 5x more patients safely, avoided further fines, and earned SOC 2 attestation. Clinicians trusted the system, reducing burnout by 30% through reliable AI assistance.
